title: Amy Madigan Ascends to No. 1 in Best Supporting Actress Oscar Race
text: After winning the Critics Choice Award for her role as Aunt Gladys in the horror film Weapons, Amy Madigan has jumped to the top of Oscar predictions for Best Supporting Actress. The 75-year-old veteran actress now carries a 90 percent probability of receiving a nomination. Current Oscar Frontrunners: Madigan faces competition from Teyana Taylor (One Battle After Another) at 89 percent, Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas (Sentimental Value) at 81 percent, and Wunmi Mosaku (Sinners) at 60 percent. Notably, Ariana Grande has dropped from the top five contenders. Historic Achievement Potential: A nomination would mark a remarkable comeback for Madigan, whose previous Oscar nod came 40 years ago for Twice in a Lifetime (1985). A victory would make her the second-oldest winner in the category's history. Her husband, actor Ed Harris, reportedly encouraged her: You wait and see, something's gonna come up and people are going to recognize her talent. Standout Performance: Despite having only 14 minutes of screen time, Madigan's portrayal has captivated audiences. Director Zach Cregger reflected on her impact, saying she's been doing sensational work for a really long time, but somehow flying under the radar.
